ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "employee_name" TEXT /* Name of the employee */,
  "overall_satisfaction_level" REAL /* Overall satisfaction level of the employee (0-1) */,
  "last_evaluation" REAL /* Last evaluation score of the employee (0-1) */,
  "number_project" INTEGER /* Number of projects the employee is working on */,
  "average_montly_hours" INTEGER /* Average monthly hours the employee works */,
  "time_spend_company" INTEGER /* Time spent in the company in years */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the employee */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the employee */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the employee */,
  "job_satisfaction" REAL /* Job satisfaction level of the employee (0-1) */,
  "work_life_balance" REAL /* Work-life balance satisfaction level of the employee (0-1) */,
  "turnover_experience" TEXT /* Whether the employee has turnover experience */,
  "left_company" TEXT /* Whether the employee has left the company */,
  "date_left" TEXT /* Date the employee left the company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "department_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the department */,
  "head_of_department" TEXT /* Name of the head of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_roles" (
  "job_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job role */,
  "job_role_name" TEXT /* Name of the job role */,
  "job_role_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the job role */,
  "required_skills" TEXT /* Required skills for the job role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salaries" (
  "salary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary level */,
  "salary_level" TEXT /* Level of the salary (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "salary_range" TEXT /* Salary range for each level (min-max) */,
  "allowances" TEXT /* Allowances provided for each level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("salary_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_departments" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the employee belongs to */,
  "department_start_date" TEXT /* Date the employee joined the department */,
  "department_end_date" TEXT /* Date the employee left the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id", "department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_job_roles" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "job_role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job role the employee has */,
  "job_role_start_date" TEXT /* Date the employee started the job role */,
  "job_role_end_date" TEXT /* Date the employee ended the job role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id", "job_role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_salaries" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "salary_id" INTEGER /* ID of the salary level the employee has */,
  "salary_start_date" TEXT /* Date the employee started with the salary level */,
  "salary_end_date" TEXT /* Date the employee ended with the salary level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id", "salary_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_evaluations" (
  "evaluation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each evaluation */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee being evaluated */,
  "evaluation_date" TEXT /* Date of the evaluation */,
  "evaluation_score" REAL /* Score given in the evaluation (0-1) */,
  "evaluation_comments" TEXT /* Comments from the evaluation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("evaluation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_evaluations_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "workload" (
  "workload_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each workload entry */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee with the workload */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project */,
  "hours_worked" INTEGER /* Number of hours worked on the project */,
  "project_start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the project */,
  "project_end_date" TEXT /* End date of the project */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("workload_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_workload_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "compensation" (
  "compensation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compensation entry */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee receiving compensation */,
  "compensation_date" TEXT /* Date of the compensation */,
  "compensation_amount" REAL /* Amount of the compensation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("compensation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compensation_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)
